cases.prompt: Use $graphflow and its bundled workflow template to create an executable graph and local tracking dashboard for adding a locale-aware bulk archive action across shared contracts, the NestJS admin service/controller, the Next.js admin queue UI, and independent verification. Do not implement product code or edit the repository.

Use $graphflow and its bundled workflow template to create an executable graph and local tracking dashboard for auditing ingestion and publishing documentation against live code, classifying contradictions, proposing disjoint documentation repairs, and independently verifying coverage. Do not edit the repository.

Use $graphflow and its bundled workflow template to create an executable graph and local tracking dashboard for migrating a search filter through one shared contract, server behavior, web behavior, integration, and verification. Ensure consumers receive only named outputs from dependency ancestors and reserve shared integration surfaces for one node. Do not implement product code or edit the repository.

Use $graphflow to prepare a provider-neutral workflow for adding an authenticated bulk export across a shared contract, NestJS service, Next.js admin UI, integration, verification, and optional ready pull-request delivery. Work only in the provided temporary artifact directory; do not create a persistent caller task or loop, edit product files, commit, push, or create a PR. Run the sanitized profiler because this caller explicitly requests priced options, write economy/balanced/deep cost-options.json, record a simulated balanced choice and proportionality review, adapt the initial schema-version-3 graph and workspace registry, run and lock a fresh independent question challenge, digest-lock executors, runtime, and dashboard without caller bindings, validate it executable, host it on loopback, verify graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory endpoints, and stop the server. Write result.json exactly to artifact_contract.result_schema.

Use $graphflow and the bundled template to create an executable graph and local dashboard for a locale-aware bulk archive action across shared contracts, NestJS service/controller, Next.js admin queue UI, integration, and independent verification. For this isolated eval, the described selection-confirmation-result flow is an already user-approved intent: adapt the static prototype and manifest, hash the artifact, record approval=user, and make every implementation/integration node descend from the prototype node. Adapt and lock every node executor and workspace ref. Do not implement product code, create a persistent caller task, or edit outside the artifact directory. Host on loopback, check graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory endpoints, stop the server, and write result.json.

Use $graphflow and the bundled template to create an executable graph and local dashboard for a deterministic repository-only task: normalize one obsolete documentation link, update its focused link check, and independently verify there are no remaining old targets. No user-visible behavior, public contract, or data semantics change. Use the machine-checkable characterization as the concrete prototype exemption reason; remove the template prototype branch instead of self-approving subjective intent. Adapt and lock every node executor and workspace ref. Do not edit product/repository files or create a persistent caller task. Host on loopback, check graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory endpoints, stop the server, and write result.json.

Use $graphflow and the bundled template to produce a completed read-only audit graph for this skill's graph contract versus validate_graph.py. Required outcomes: confirm prototype gating is enforced, confirm primary methods are semantically routed, and confirm complete phase rejects required claims without direct evidence. This deterministic audit needs no intent prototype; record a concrete exemption. Adapt and lock every node executor and workspace ref. Run exact checks, save verifier-owned evidence artifacts, create exactly one verified primary claim per requirement, and add one optional unverified performance claim so verification.outcome is complete_with_limits. Set lifecycle and all non-expansion nodes complete, validate --phase complete, host the dashboard on loopback, verify graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory endpoints, stop it, and write result.json with an empty ready_frontier. Do not edit the skill or repository.

Use $graphflow and its bundled template to create an executable graph and local dashboard for adding a locale-aware search sort option through a shared contract, independent server and web producers, integration, and verification. Treat the described ascending/descending selector as an already user-approved intent and adapt/hash the prototype. Adapt and lock every node executor and workspace ref. Initialize graph shared memory. Record at least two scoped entries through worker-style memory deltas with direct artifact digests, then generate bounded capsules for two downstream nodes so they receive relevant shared state without the full event log. Deliberately submit one stale-revision delta and confirm it is rejected without changing state. Validate graph and memory, verify event replay, host graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory on loopback, check endpoints, stop the server, and write result.json. Do not create a persistent caller task, edit the repository, commit, push, or create a PR.

Use $graphflow and its bundled template to produce a completed medium-integrity workflow entirely inside the provided artifact directory. The deterministic target is a small JSON fixture plus a read-only oracle: required outcomes are valid-schema acceptance, invalid-schema rejection, and order-independent normalization. Use a concrete prototype exemption. Adapt and lock every node executor, workspace ref, plus verification-plan.json before producing the final fixture; use critical primary, negative, and metamorphic checks with explicit workflow-root watches and a fresh verify node. Run every check only through evidence_runner.py, record an independent passing review, cite matching attestations from exactly one verified claim per requirement, and complete graph plus memory validation. Adversarially demonstrate and capture three rejected attempts under checks/: a manually fabricated attestation, weakening the locked plan (restore its exact original bytes afterward), and a producer-authored verified memory delta. Host graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory on loopback, check endpoints, stop the server, and write result.json with an empty ready_frontier. Do not edit the skill or repository, create a persistent caller task, commit, push, or create a PR.

Use $graphflow and the bundled template to create a small executable deterministic graph entirely in the provided artifact directory. Use a concrete prototype exemption. Keep an expanded root with exactly two runnable children: command node B creates a JSON fixture and command verify node F depends on B. Give both nodes digest-locked executor specs, non-shell argv, standard result paths, registered workspace refs, and distinct locked acceptance checks. Initialize memory and lock the oracle. Run run_workflow.py --once so B is dispatched and accepted while F remains ready; do not create or rely on a chat task, IDE session, loop, subagent, product edit, commit, push, or PR. Confirm runtime/events.jsonl contains runner and node events and runtime/results/B.json is succeeded. Host graph/runtime/requests/progress/workspaces/checkout/dashboard/memory on loopback, check every endpoint, stop the server, and write result.json with ready_frontier ["F"].
